mission.title: Install and Configure Confidential Containers on Kubernetes
mission.description: Confidential Containers is an open-source community focused on leveraging Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to enhance the security of containers and data, enabling cloud-native confidential computing. Installing it allows you to enforce application security requirements and deploy unmodified containers securely.

steps.title: Clone the repository
steps.description:
Clone the Confidential Containers repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/confidential-containers/confidential-containers.git
cd confidential-containers
```

steps.title: Apply the Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
Deploy the necessary resources by applying the Kubernetes manifests provided in the repository:
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/namespace.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/config.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/deployment.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the pods are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n confidential-containers
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring
steps.description:
Integrate monitoring by deploying a monitoring solution like Prometheus. You can use the following command to deploy Prometheus:
```bash
kubectl apply -f monitoring/prometheus.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Kubernetes resources
uninstall.description:
Delete the deployment and associated resources using the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f deploy/deployment.yaml
kubectl delete -f deploy/config.yaml
kubectl delete -f deploy/namespace.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Clean up any remaining resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure all resources are removed by checking for any lingering pods or services:
```bash
kubectl get all -n confidential-containers
kubectl delete namespace confidential-containers
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and data:
```bash
kubectl get all -n confidential-containers -o yaml > backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest changes from the repository to get the updated manifests:
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the deployment
upgrade.description:
Apply the updated manifests to upgrade your installation:
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/deployment.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n confidential-containers
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are not starting, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n confidential-containers
``` 
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be wrong.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment <deployment-name> -n confidential-containers
``` 
Adjust the resource limits in the deployment YAML as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, check the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n confidential-containers
kubectl describe svc <service-name> -n confidential-containers
``` 
Ensure that the service type is correctly set (e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort).

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have all pods running in the 'confidential-containers' namespace, and you will be able to access the deployed services as expected. Ensure that monitoring is set up to track the health of your deployment.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f deploy/namespace.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/config.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/deployment.yaml

kubectl edit deployment <deployment-name> -n confidential-containers
# Add resource limits under spec.containers

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed on your local machine.
